title: App Manifest
description: Defines a single app hosted by the simple-apps shared platform. Every app directory must contain a manifest.json that satisfies this schema.

name.description: Unique machine-readable slug for this app. Lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens and underscores only. Must match the directory name under apps/. Example: 'todos', 'razorblades', 'books'.

label.description: Human-readable display name shown in the navigation bar and page titles. Example: 'To-Do List', 'Razor Blade Tracker'.

version.description: Semantic version string (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). Increment PATCH for bug fixes, MINOR for new features, MAJOR for breaking changes. Example: '1.0.0'.

description.description: One-sentence plain-English description of what the app does. Shown in the platform home page / app picker.

icon.description: Emoji or short string used as the app icon in navigation and headings. Optional. Example: '✓', '🪒', '📚'.

url_prefix.description: URL path prefix under which this app's frontend is served. Must start with '/' and match the app name. The platform mounts the frontend dist at this path. Example: '/todos'.

api_prefix.description: URL path prefix for this app's REST API endpoints. Defaults to '/api/<name>' if omitted. Example: '/api/todos'.

db_path.description: Path to the app's SQLite database file, relative to the repository root. The platform creates the file and parent directory if they do not exist. This path should live inside a volume-mounted directory so data persists across container restarts. Example: 'apps/todos/data/todos.db'.

router_module.description: Python dotted module path to the FastAPI APIRouter object named 'router' inside the app package. The platform imports this module at startup and mounts the router at api_prefix. Example: 'apps.todos.router'.

frontend_dist.description: Path to the compiled React/Vite dist/ folder, relative to the repository root. This directory is volume-mounted so new builds can be deployed without an image rebuild. The platform serves static files from here at url_prefix. Example: 'apps/todos/frontend/dist'.

events.description: Groundwork declaration for the future inter-app event bus. Both arrays are currently unused by the platform but are parsed at startup for validation and future routing. See docs/event-bus.md for the full design.

emits.description: Event type strings this app will publish to the bus. Example: ['todo.created', 'todo.completed'].

consumes.description: Event type strings this app subscribes to from the bus. Example: ['books.item.completed'].

items.description: Dot-namespaced event type. Must match an event declared in the 'emits' list of another app.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs for app-specific configuration that does not fit the standard fields. The platform ignores this object but passes it through to the router on startup.
